questions.question: Entrepreneurship can best be described as ______.
options: a process that requires setting up a business
taking a significant risk in a business context
a process involving innovation, new products or services, and value creation
questions.explanation: All three options are correct. Entrepreneurship means starting a business, taking risks, and creating new value.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 1 - Meaning and Nature of Entrepreneurship

questions.question: If Kojo starts a new service station, it is an example of a/an ______.
questions.explanation: Service stations already exist (existing concept), but Kojo is starting a new one (new business).
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 1 - Types of Entrepreneurial Ventures

questions.question: The first person who introduced the term "entrepreneur" is ______.
questions.explanation: Richard Cantillon was the first person to use the word entrepreneur to describe someone who takes risks.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 1 - Historical Development of Entrepreneurship

questions.question: ______ entrepreneurship is defined as entrepreneurial actions undertaken by individuals living in poverty, and as such, concerned about their survival.
questions.explanation: Subsistence means just getting by. These entrepreneurs start businesses to survive, not to get rich.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Types of Entrepreneurship

questions.question: As a new company grows, the entrepreneur will need to ______.
options: be responsive to changes in the market
questions.explanation: Growing a business needs all three: respond to changes, control money, and build a good team.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 4 - Growth and Development of Small Businesses

questions.question: ______ entrepreneurship is an approach by individuals, groups, start-up companies or entrepreneurs, in which they develop, fund and implement solutions to social, cultural, or environmental issues.
questions.explanation: Social entrepreneurs want to solve problems like poverty or pollution, not just make money.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Social Entrepreneurship

questions.question: The characteristics of an individual that causes consistent patterns in that individual behavior over time is referred to as entrepreneurial ______.
questions.explanation: Your personality is what makes you act the same way again and again over time.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Entrepreneurial Personality

questions.question: Entrepreneurs would be best described as risk ______.
questions.explanation: Entrepreneurs take calculated risks. They are not afraid to try things that might fail.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Risk Bearing in Entrepreneurship

questions.question: The following are ways to avoid pitfalls of small business failure except ______.
options: set your business along with the competition
questions.explanation: Do not just copy your competitors. Be different. The other options help you avoid failure.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 4 - Avoiding Small Business Failure

questions.question: An individual who actively engages in creating a new venture either alone or with someone and is expected to be the owner or part owner of this start-up is a ______ entrepreneur.
questions.explanation: Nascent means just beginning. These people are in the process of starting a business.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Types of Entrepreneurs

questions.question: An entrepreneur who has internal locus of control ______.
options: has a high need for achievement
believes that success depends on his/her own actions
believes fate is a powerful force
questions.explanation: Internal locus means you believe your own hard work controls your success, not luck or fate.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Psychological Characteristics of Entrepreneurs

questions.question: An entrepreneur who owns more than one business at a time is called ______.
questions.explanation: Portfolio means collection. These entrepreneurs have a collection of businesses all at once.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Portfolio Entrepreneurs

questions.question: Mr. Agunde's belief in his capability to perform a task successfully is known as ______.
questions.explanation: Self-efficacy is confidence in your own ability to get a job done well.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Entrepreneurial Self-Efficacy

questions.question: Someone who improves an existing business using creativity and opportunity identification can be considered an/a ______.
questions.explanation: Intrapreneur means entrepreneur inside a company. They innovate within an existing business.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Intrapreneurship

questions.question: Which of these statements is true about entrepreneurship?
questions.explanation: Entrepreneurship needs both coming up with new ideas (creativity) and turning them into real products (innovation).
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 3 - Creativity and Innovation in Entrepreneurship

questions.question: Creation, a type of creative behavior entails ______.
options: an act of joining two previously unrelated things
modifying an existing product or service
identifying and evaluating various product alternatives
questions.explanation: Creation means making something completely new, not just changing or combining old things.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 3 - Types of Creative Behaviour

questions.question: Offering tangible rewards to those generating ideas enhances creativity.
questions.explanation: True. When people get real rewards for good ideas, they are more motivated to be creative.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 3 - Encouraging Creativity

questions.question: An entrepreneur is a person who has decided to take control of his future and become self-employed, whether by creating his own unique business or working as a member of a team.
questions.explanation: True. An entrepreneur takes charge of their own future and works for themselves.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 1 - Definition of an Entrepreneur

questions.question: Personality is defined as the characteristics of an individual that cause consistent patterns in that individual's behaviour overtime.
questions.explanation: True. Personality is what makes you act in steady, predictable ways over time.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Entrepreneurial Personality

questions.question: A trait is expressed as a dimension on which a person can be measured.
questions.explanation: True. Traits like confidence or risk-taking can be measured on a scale.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Traits of Entrepreneurs

questions.question: The word entrepreneurship was derived from a Greek word "entrepreneur" meaning to undertake.
questions.explanation: False. It comes from the French word 'entreprendre', not Greek.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 1 - Historical Development of Entrepreneurship

questions.question: The connection between risk and entrepreneurship was established in the 17th Century.
questions.explanation: True. Richard Cantillon linked risk and entrepreneurship in the 17th century.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 1 - Cantillon and Risk in Entrepreneurship

questions.question: The entrepreneurial process does not include the "entrepreneur".
questions.explanation: False. The entrepreneur is a key part of the process, along with opportunity, resources, and organization.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - The Entrepreneurial Process

questions.question: One of the basic rules of Brainstorming is to generate, do not evaluate.
questions.explanation: True. In brainstorming, you just throw out ideas first. Judge them later.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 2 - Brainstorming Technique

questions.question: A business plan should not be prepared by the entrepreneur but rather by a professional.
questions.explanation: False. The entrepreneur should write the plan. Professionals can help, but the entrepreneur knows the business best.
questions.reference: Unit 3, Session 1 - Responsibility for Preparing a Business Plan

questions.question: An entrepreneur is a person driven by certain forces which include the need for achievement and to escape authority of others. This definition was given by ______.
questions.explanation: Psychologists study the mind and motivation. They say people become entrepreneurs to achieve goals and be free from bosses.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Psychological Approach to Entrepreneurship

questions.question: Joe is resourceful, quick thinker, and his level of restlessness makes him move from one business to another. Joe could be referred to as ______ entrepreneur.
questions.explanation: Serial entrepreneurs start a business, leave it, then start another one over and over.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Serial Entrepreneurs

questions.question: Three reasons why people become entrepreneurs are as follows, except ______.
questions.explanation: People start businesses to be free, follow their ideas, and make money. Managing others is not a main reason.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 1 - Why People Start Businesses

questions.question: Which of the following is not a characteristic of a successful entrepreneur?
questions.explanation: Risk averse means avoiding risk. Successful entrepreneurs take risks, they do not avoid them.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Characteristics of Successful Entrepreneurs

questions.question: Which of the following is not a personality characteristic of entrepreneurs?
questions.explanation: Positive push comes from society, not from your personality. The others are personality traits.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 3 - Personality vs Sociological Factors

questions.question: Mr. Oheneba Gyebi developed the impetus and momentum for entrepreneurship based on the education and the knowledge acquired. Which sociological approach to entrepreneurship is this?
questions.explanation: Positive push means good things like education and skills push you toward starting a business.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 3 - Sociological Approaches to Entrepreneurship

questions.question: Which of the entrepreneurship environment is affected by the taste and preferences of consumers?
questions.explanation: Tastes and preferences come from society and culture. That is the socio-cultural environment.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 4 - External Environment of Entrepreneurship

questions.question: Which of the following is not part of the reasons for starting your own business as outlined by Birley and Westhead (1994)?
questions.explanation: Entrepreneurs want independence, not dependence. Needing to depend on others is not a reason to start a business.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 1 - Motives for Business Start-ups

questions.question: If a business starts with no backlog of problems, the business is said to have a ______.
questions.explanation: Clean sheet means starting fresh with no old problems or debts from the past.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 5 - Buying an Existing Business

questions.question: According to Hisrich and Peters, which of the following is the largest source for new business idea?
questions.explanation: Consumers are the biggest source. Listening to what customers want gives you the best business ideas.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 2 - Sources of Business Ideas

questions.question: Which of the following is not a method of generating new business idea?
questions.explanation: Distribution channels are a source of ideas, not a method. Methods are things like brainstorming and focus groups.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 2 - Idea Generation Techniques

questions.question: Which phase of new business venture begins with an idea for the venture and ends when the doors of the venture are opened for business?
questions.explanation: Pre start-up is everything that happens before you open your doors, from the first idea to getting ready.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 3 - Phases of Venture Creation

questions.question: Mr. John Mensah (a Chartered Accountant) bought 30% shares to become part owner of ReGAS Financial Services, a company owned by two professional accountants. Mr. Mensah can be classified under which options for small business buyers?
questions.explanation: Buy-in means an outsider buys part of an existing business. Mr. Mensah was not an owner before, so he bought in.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 5 - Options for Small Business Buyers

questions.question: Which section of a business plan establishes a favourable first impression of the owners and the business, and succeed in securing financing?
questions.explanation: The Executive Summary is the first thing people read. It must sell your business idea to get funding.
questions.reference: Unit 3, Session 2 - Components of the Business Plan

questions.question: Which type of franchising can also be referred to as Dealership?
questions.explanation: Product distribution franchising is also called a dealership, like car dealers or fuel stations.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 6 - Types of Franchising
